frameworks.name: economic decision-making under uncertainty (risk vs ambiguity)
frameworks.one_line: distinguishes preferences under known vs unknown outcome probabilities

frameworks.name: cumulative lifetime stressor exposure (STRAIN)
frameworks.one_line: links psychosocial stress history to ambiguity-aversion individual differences

core_question: Does cumulative lifetime stressor exposure predict individual differences in ambiguity aversion?
key_assumptions: risk and ambiguity preferences are dissociable economic constructs
STRAIN validly quantifies lifetime stressor exposure
early-life stressors are separable from later-life stressors
manipulations: outcome-probability information (known = risk vs unknown = ambiguity)
lifetime stressor exposure measured across early and later life
key_findings: greater lifetime stressor exposure associated with higher ambiguity aversion
early-life stressor exposure was particularly predictive
stressor exposure did not predict risk preferences
effect replicated across two independent studies
interpretations: cumulative psychosocial stress is an individual-difference factor specifically shaping aversion to unknown probabilities
ambiguity aversion and risk aversion have distinct origins in stress history
